title: campanula glomerata subsp. oblongifolioides
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. oblongifolioides, also known as the clustered bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Campanula glomerata subsp. oblongifolioides include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce pain, and combat infections.
